skill.name: wan-2-7
skill.description: Generate text-to-video with Wan 2.7 (Wan-AI's flagship motion model) on RunComfy. Documents Wan 2.7's strengths (multi-reference conditioning, audio-driven lip-sync via `audio_url`, smoother transitions, prompt expansion), the duration / resolution / aspect-ratio schema, and when to route to HappyHorse 1.0 / Seedance 2.0 / Kling / LTX 2 instead. Calls `runcomfy run wan-ai/wan-2-7/text-to-video` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "wan", "wan 2.7", "wan-2-7", "wan video", or any explicit ask to generate video with this model.
skill.summary: Generate text-to-video with Wan 2.7 via RunComfy CLI — audio-driven lip-sync, multi-reference conditioning, and smooth motion physics.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 72 patterns including backtick execution, hardcoded URLs, path traversal, hidden file access, and weak crypto. After evaluation, all findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The 'backtick execution' matches are markdown inline code formatting. Hardcoded URLs are legitimate documentation links to runcomfy.com. Path traversal and hidden file references are example paths in documentation. Weak crypto matches are spurious text pattern matches. The skill is a well-documented video generation wrapper with a clear Security & Privacy section explaining token storage, input boundary handling, and outbound endpoints.

low_findings.title: Documentation references external CLI invocation
low_findings.description: The skill documents usage of `runcomfy run wan-ai/wan-2-7/text-to-video` CLI commands. This is expected behavior for a skill that wraps an external API. No code injection vector exists as the CLI handles JSON input directly without shell interpolation.

low_findings.title: Documentation references token storage path
low_findings.description: Line 181 documents that `runcomfy login` stores tokens at `~/.config/runcomfy/token.json` with mode 0600. This is a security BEST PRACTICE disclosure, not a vulnerability — the skill author is transparently documenting where credentials are stored.

content.user_title: Generate Cinematic Videos with Wan 2.7 AI
content.value_statement: Creating professional text-to-video content requires understanding model-specific parameters and capabilities. This skill provides guided access to Wan 2.7's audio-driven lip-sync and multi-reference conditioning through the RunComfy CLI.
actual_capabilities: Generate text-to-video using Wan 2.7 with configurable duration (2-15s), resolution (720p/1080p), and aspect ratio (16:9, 9:16, 1:1, 4:3, 3:4)
Drive lip-sync generation by supplying an audio_url parameter (WAV/MP3, 3-30s, up to 15MB)
Apply multi-reference conditioning with up to 5 reference media items (image, video, or voice)
Control output determinism via seed parameter for consistent variants
Toggle prompt expansion on/off for literal or auto-enhanced prompt interpretation
Route requests to the correct sibling model (HappyHorse 1.0, Seedance 2.0, Kling, LTX 2) when Wan 2.7 is not the best fit
limitations: Maximum video duration is 15 seconds — longer narratives require stitching multiple generations
Resolution ceiling is 1080p — no native 4K output available
Only 5 documented aspect ratios are supported
Audio input restricted to 3-30 seconds, 15MB max, WAV/MP3 formats only

use_cases.title: Marketing teams creating lip-synced video ads
use_cases.description: Generate spokesperson videos with custom voiceover tracks for product campaigns, social media ads, and brand content.

use_cases.title: Content creators producing multi-language video variants
use_cases.description: Create the same video scene with different audio tracks for international audiences, dubbing campaigns, or localized content.

use_cases.title: Developers integrating AI video into applications
use_cases.description: Build automated video generation pipelines using the RunComfy CLI with deterministic seeds for reproducible outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic text-to-video generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 5-second 1080p 16:9 video of: [describe your scene with camera motion, subject, and lighting]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick video generation with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Lip-synced spokesperson video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 9:16 vertical video of a spokesperson delivering my audio track at [URL]. Use medium close-up, locked tripod, warm key light, shallow depth of field.

prompt_templates.title: Product showcase with cinematic motion
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 1080p video: Cinematic medium shot of [product] on [surface], soft studio lighting, slow subtle camera push-in, shallow depth of field, premium commercial look.

prompt_templates.title: Literal prompt with negative constraints
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a video with enable_prompt_expansion disabled. Prompt: '[exact description]'. Negative prompt: 'no subtitles, no watermark, no flicker, no distorted hands'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Strict brand or creative control scenarios

output_examples.input: Generate a 5s 1080p 16:9 video of a barista pulling espresso with rising steam in morning light
output_examples.output: Returns a video file in the specified output directory showing slow-motion espresso extraction with visible steam dynamics, warm color grading, and shallow depth of field. File saved as MP4.

output_examples.input: Create a 9:16 lip-synced spokesperson video using my voiceover.mp3
output_examples.output: Returns a vertical video with the spokesperson's mouth movements synchronized to the supplied audio track, locked camera framing, and broadcast-quality lighting.

output_examples.input: Generate product showcase with enable_prompt_expansion disabled and negative prompts
output_examples.output: Returns a video adhering strictly to the input prompt description, with watermarks, subtitles, and visual artifacts explicitly excluded by the negative prompt.

best_practices: Front-load camera and motion descriptions in prompts (e.g., 'slow dolly in', 'locked tripod') for clearer motion guidance
Use one primary action per clip to avoid conflicting motion instructions
Reuse the same seed when iterating on a prompt to maintain visual consistency across variants
anti_patterns: Describing static frames without motion vocabulary — results in vague or minimal movement
Using vague negative prompts like 'no bad colors' — provide concrete exclusions like 'no watermark, no subtitles'
Submitting audio outside the 3-30s / 15MB / WAV-MP3 specification — requests will be rejected

faq.question: What is the maximum video duration Wan 2.7 can generate?
faq.answer: Wan 2.7 supports durations from 2 to 15 seconds in whole-second increments. For longer content, generate multiple clips and stitch them together.

faq.question: Can I provide my own audio for lip-sync generation?
faq.answer: Yes. Supply an audio_url parameter pointing to a WAV or MP3 file that is 3-30 seconds long and under 15MB. The model will synchronize the subject's lip movements to your audio.

faq.question: How does the RunComfy CLI handle my API token?
faq.answer: After running `runcomfy login`, your token is stored at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with file permissions set to owner-only read/write (0600). For CI or containerized environments, set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable instead.

faq.question: Is prompt expansion always enabled?
faq.answer: By default, enable_prompt_expansion is true, meaning short prompts are automatically rewritten by the model. Set it to false in your JSON input if you need literal interpretation of your prompt text.

faq.question: What aspect ratios does Wan 2.7 support?
faq.answer: Wan 2.7 supports five aspect ratios: 16:9 (landscape), 9:16 (vertical/portrait), 1:1 (square), 4:3 (standard), and 3:4 (portrait standard).

faq.question: How do I get reproducible results across multiple generations?
faq.answer: Use the seed parameter with an integer value between 0 and 2^31-1. Using the same seed with the same prompt will produce consistent output. Change the seed to introduce variation.
